compiled_state.title: As a DSO user, archived tickets are excluded from ticket list but available via ticket show
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Introduce an ARCHIVED event type in the ticket reducer, add filtering logic so ticket list excludes archived tickets while ticket show includes them, and implement transitive dependency traversal to determine archive eligibility.
**Why**: Closed tickets with no relationship to open work account for significant reducer overhead. Archiving them removes them from the hot path while preserving them for reference.
**Scope**:
- IN: ARCHIVED event type in reducer, list/show filtering, transitive graph traversal algorithm (depends_on/blocks edges, ignoring relates_to), ARCHIVED event type in ticket-lib.sh allowed enum
- OUT: Bulk archival execution (Story 4), CI scheduling, Jira bridge changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the reducer skips tickets with an ARCHIVED event when invoked by ticket list, but includes them when invoked by ticket show
  <- Satisfies: 'The reducer skips tickets with an ARCHIVED event when invoked by ticket list, but includes them when invoked by ticket show'
- When this story is complete, archive eligibility is determined by transitive dependency traversal: starting from every open ticket, following all depends_on/blocks edges recursively — reachable closed tickets are ineligible, relates_to edges are ignored
  <- Satisfies: 'Archive eligibility uses transitive dependency traversal'
- When this story is complete, the chosen approach for ARCHIVED (status value vs. separate event type with flag) is documented and all affected consumers are updated
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Integration] The new ARCHIVED event type must be added to the allowed event_type enum in ticket-lib.sh (CREATE|STATUS|COMMENT|LINK|UNLINK|SNAPSHOT|SYNC|REVERT|EDIT). Without this, write_commit_event() will reject ARCHIVED events.
- [Integration] ticket-graph.py has tombstone-awareness: missing directories are treated as closed, and .tombstone.json files are read for status. Explicitly decide how archived tickets appear to ticket-graph.py.
- [Performance] Transitive graph traversal is O(V+E) — at 1,000 tickets with dense dependency graphs, ensure traversal completes in acceptable time
- [Maintainability] Story 1 also modifies ticket-reducer.py — coordinate to avoid merge conflicts
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Story complete: ARCHIVED event type with list/show filtering + transitive archive eligibility traversal. All tests GREEN.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
